title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Evidence Ladder
summary: A volunteer digital library asks for a word-count script but has not defined the report user, authorized input, counting policy, failure behavior, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the evidence ladder increment in Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence chart…
objectives: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.
Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-evidence-ladder — Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_outcomes_repo_evidence_3(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-evidence-ladder-pczmob
# Competency: bookbot-evidence-ladder.
# Case 3500: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_evidence_ladder_pczmob(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pczmob = "pczmob-bomzcp-3500"
    assert evidence_variant_pczmob
    evidence_axes_pczmob = (
        "corpuspczmob",
        "policybomzcp",
        "revision3500",
        "commandpczmob3500",
        "reportbomzcp3500",
        "test3500pczmob",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pczmob)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Bookbot team handles case 1857 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.
